rulings.Case Title: KELSTIN GROUP, INC. DBA PACIFIC CREDIT SERVICES VS. JERREL KRISTOPHER STANCIL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment Balance With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KELSTIN GROUP, INC. DBA PACIFIC CREDIT SERVICES' Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment Balance. Plaintiff's mistitled "motion to amend judgment balance" is denied. By judgment entered October 18, 2019, the Court granted a default judgment in favor of plaintiff in the amount of $4,609.13. Plaintiff now seeks to set aside that judgment under Section 473(b) of the Code of Civil Procedure, contending that it inadvertently failed to appear at the prove up hearing set for January 9, 2020, but did appear the next day and found out that the case had been dismissed due to its failure to appear. However, no such scheduled hearing or dismissal appears on the court's docket. Plaintiff admits that "[t]he amount on the judgment was listed incorrectly when submitted to the courts." (Solomon Decl. par. 3.) It now belatedly seeks to amend the judgment to reflect the "correct" judgment balance, which it apparently contends (without any supporting evidence) should be $7,419.13, the amount it originally sought in October 2019. Plaintiff's motion is untimely, having been filed more than one year after the expiration of the 6-month period specified in section 473(b), which is jurisdictional. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 473(b) [application "shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken"]; Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["The six-month time limit for granting statutory relief is jurisdictional and the court may not consider a motion for relief made after that period has elapsed."].) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. VS. REGIONAL DEMOLITION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Regional Demolition, Inc., To Served Initial Responses To Set One Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 2, 2. PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Regional Demolition, Inc., To Served Initial Responses To Set One Form Interrogatories: Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. See motion 1 above. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. VS. REGIONAL DEMOLITION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 2, 1. PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s Motion To Deem Matters Admitted: Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The requests for admission might not have been properly served. They were served by email. CCP 1010.6, subdivision e)(1), says that before first serving by email, the serving party must confirm by email or telephone the electronic service address of counsel being served. Was that done? If not, does it matter if the papers are emailed to the appropriate email address? Defendant's argument that plaintiff did not meet and confer is rejected. Since no responses were served, there was no requirement to meet and confer. (Demyer v. Costa Mesa Mobile Home Estates (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 393, 395.) Additionally, plaintiff met and conferred with a letter to which defendant did not respond. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 373-375 CAPP STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. 373 CAPP STREET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Erroneous Stipulation And Order Of Reference To Special Master Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Withdraw Non-Existent Stipulation To Same By Defendants 373 Capp Street, Llc, Urban Green Investments, Llc, Peter W. Hagist, And David P. Mccloskey
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 7, DEFENDANTS PETER HAGIST, DAVID MCCLOSKEY, 373 CAPP STREET, LLC, AND URBAN GREEN INVESTMENTS, LLC's Motion To Vacate Erroneous Stipulation And Order Of Reference To Special Master Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Withdraw Non-Existent Stipulation To Same By Defendants 373 Capp Street, Llc, Urban Green Investments, Llc, Peter W. Hagist, And David P. Mccloskey With Court Reporter Fee. Defendants' (373 Capp Street, LLC, Urban Green Investments, LLC, Peter W. Hagist, and David P. McCloskey) motion to vacate the stipulation and order for reference entered on February 4, 2021 is granted. The Court ordered reference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 638. A reference under that statute requires "the agreement of the parties," which did not occur in this case. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 638.) "The superior court has no power to assign matters to a referee for decision without explicit statutory authorization." (Aetna Life Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1986) 182 Cal.App.3d 431, 435.) The Court therefore vacates the order. The Court believes that this case is fairly characterized as a construction defect action. Discovery references in such cases are customary and useful. This order is without prejudice to a party making a motion for reference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 639 if the circumstances warrant that relief. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ROYAL CUCKOO INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYDS LONDON POLICY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 11, DEFENDANT CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYDS LONDON POLICY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The moving party failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 because there is no declaration stating that the parties met and conferred prior to this filing. Plaintiff's opposition confirms that there was no compliance with this requirement. The demurrer is therefore off calendar and the parties are ordered to immediately meet and confer. Defendant's response to the complaint is now due June 23, 2021. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ZAIF INC. VS. BINANCE HOLDINGS LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Binance Holdings Ltd.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 26, 2021, Line 3, DEFENDANT BINANCE HOLDINGS LTD.'s Motion To Stay Discovery Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants must respond without objection at this time to discovery requests that might reasonably lead to evidence that would be admissible in opposing the pending motions to quash or dismiss and are not otherwise objectionable. At least many of the discovery requests appear to the court as requests that might reasonably lead to evidence that would be admissible in opposing the motions. Discovery requests that are not requests that might reasonably lead to evidence that would be admissible in opposing the pending motions can be objected to at this time on that basis. But if the court denies the motions to quash and dismiss, then within 30 days after notice of the orders denying both motions, defendant will have to amend any responses objected to on that basis because objections on that basis will no longer be valid once both motions are denied. The court does not decide in response to this stay motion which discovery requests might reasonably lead to evidence that would be admissible in opposing the motions to quash or dismiss, and which discovery requests, if any, are not requests that might reasonably lead to evidence that would be admissible in opposing the motions to quash or dismiss. Nor does the court in response to this motion decide which discovery requests might be otherwise objectionable. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
